narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings—pouring your heart into law school dreams only to have the door close feels deeply unfair, and I'm here to walk you through why our predictive model saw your path to the bar exam as riskier than we can support right now, with a 54% chance of not passing. Picture your journey like a story the model pieced together from key chapters of your background: your Year 3 grades landing you in the 4th decile stood out as the biggest signal, whispering that sustaining top performance might be tough, even more than your Year 1 in the 5th decile, which also tugged steadily toward struggle. Your LSAT score of 24 added weight to that narrative, suggesting the intense analytical demands of law might stretch you thin. Coming from a middle-income family painted a picture of fewer resources to lean on during those grueling study marathons, while your Asian background aligned in the model's eyes with patterns of higher hurdles in this arena. Your undergrad GPA of 2.6 fit into that same cautious tale, and choosing full-time law school hinted at the pressures of diving in without a slower ramp-up. It's true your gender nudged things slightly toward success in the model's view, a small bright spot amid the rest. Together, these threads wove a story of real risks outweighing the rewards, leading us to pause on your application—not because we doubt your drive, but to protect you from a path strewn with potential heartbreak. You've got strengths worth building on, like that full commitment to law; maybe bolstering those early grades or LSAT prep could rewrite this story next time. Hang in there—this isn't the end of your chapter.
